paper_title: Diversity and DNA Barcode Analysis of Chironomids (Diptera: Chironomidae) from Large Rivers in South Korea

methods.description: Process for extracting total genomic DNA from specimens to verify species identification and construct DNA barcodes.

steps.description: total genomic DNA was extracted from the thoracic pre-episternum of each specimen using a DNeasy Blood and Tissue kit (Qiagen, Hilden, Germany).
steps.searchable_summary: Extract genomic DNA from tissue samples using a commercial kit.

methods.description: Standard PCR amplification and sequencing protocols used to generate COI fragment sequences from extracted DNA.

steps.description: the target fragment of COI was amplified in 20 µL reactions containing AccuPower PCR PreMix (Bioneer Co., Daejeon, Korea), 1 U Top DNA polymerase, dNTPs (10 mM), Tris-HCl (pH 9.0), KCl (30 mM), MgCl2 (1.5 mM), 1-3 µL (5-50 ng) template DNA, and 1 µL of each primer (LCO1490 and HCO2198; 10 pM each) [28].
steps.searchable_summary: Prepare PCR reaction mixture with specified reagents and concentrations for COI amplification.

steps.description: Amplification was performed using the following thermal cycling program: 94 • C for 5 min; 35 cycles of 94 • C for 0.5 min; 48 • C for 1 min; 72 • C for 1.5 min; and a final extension at 72 • C for 10 min.
steps.searchable_summary: Execute PCR amplification using a specific thermal profile.

steps.description: The reaction products were separated on 1.5% agarose gels
steps.searchable_summary: Separate PCR products by size using agarose gel electrophoresis.
outputs: Agarose gel with separated DNA bands

steps.searchable_summary: Visualize DNA bands on an agarose gel under UV light.
inputs: Agarose gel with separated DNA bands
outputs: Image or observation of DNA bands

inputs: PCR amplified products (from gel or reaction)

steps.description: sequenced using an ABI Prism 3130 Genetic Analyzer (Applied Biosystems, Foster, CA, USA).
steps.searchable_summary: Sequence purified DNA fragments using a genetic analyzer.

methods.description: Analysis of generated COI sequences including alignment, phylogenetic tree construction, and genetic distance calculations for species identification and relationship assessment.

steps.description: The sequences were aligned using the CLC Main Workbench (version 7.8.1; CLC bio, Aarhus, Denmark)
steps.searchable_summary: Perform multiple sequence alignment of COI sequences.

steps.description: the results were crosschecked using the ClustalW algorithm in MEGA 7.0 [29].
steps.searchable_summary: Validate sequence alignment using a different algorithm (ClustalW).

steps.description: the GTR + I + Γ model of nucleotide substitution was selected as the best-fitting model for the COI sequences using jModelTest (version 2.1.10) based on the Akaike information criterion [30,31].
steps.searchable_summary: Determine the best-fit nucleotide substitution model for phylogenetic analysis using model selection software.
outputs: Selected nucleotide substitution model (GTR + I + Γ)

steps.description: For phylogenetic relationships between large Korean river chironomid species, a maximum likelihood (ML) tree was constructed using MEGA 7.0, and the GTR + I + Γ model of nucleotide substitution was selected as the best-fitting model for the COI sequences
steps.searchable_summary: Build a phylogenetic tree using the Maximum Likelihood method with a specified substitution model.
inputs: Selected nucleotide substitution model (GTR + I + Γ)

steps.description: The convergence diagnostic was calculated for 1000 generations in ML
steps.searchable_summary: Assess convergence of the Maximum Likelihood tree search.

steps.description: groups with a frequency greater than 50% were retained.
steps.searchable_summary: Filter phylogenetic tree branches based on support values (e.g., bootstrap frequency).

steps.description: Pairwise sequence divergence within and between genetic clusters was calculated using the K2P model in MEGA 7.0.
steps.searchable_summary: Compute genetic distances between sequences using the K2P model.

steps.description: Intraspecific and interspecific genetic distance analyses were performed using the K2P and maximum composite likelihood models of Tamura et al. [32], which were the best-fit nucleotide substitution and base frequency models, respectively, as indicated by MEGA 7.0.
steps.searchable_summary: Analyze genetic distances specifically for within-species and between-species comparisons using specified models.
outputs: Intraspecific and interspecific genetic distance values/summaries

steps.description: Therefore, we identified species that were higher than commonly known thresholds. Therefore, even if model-based species depictions have not been tested, there is little chance of species classification error.
steps.searchable_summary: Assign species identity based on calculated genetic distances relative to established thresholds (e.g., >3% or 4-5%).

methods.description: Process for depositing generated sequence data into a public database for accessibility.

steps.description: Sequences of COI of Korean chironomids from large rivers obtained in the current study were deposited in GenBank under accession numbers OM974370-OM974448.
steps.searchable_summary: Upload DNA sequences to a public repository like GenBank.
